concept: Clinical Finding / Health-Relevant Observation Produced by a Stated Method
description: A health-relevant observation, elicited response, measurement, image interpretation, or test result produced by a stated person, instrument, or method. A clinical finding may be normal, uncertain, incidental, or disputed. It is not a symptom, diagnosis, disease, proof of cause, clinical significance, recommendation, or permission to intervene, and its exact source artifact remains separately preserved when identity or precision matters.
sound_symbolism: Direct-evidence 'hi' opens with what was observed, 'sa' gives the observation a situated form, and final 'ro' leaves its interpretation open. The sound records contact without closing the clinical conclusion.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'shia ha hisaro sirami.' (They record this clinical finding.) Name the observer, method, conditions, time, uncertainty, and source record where relevant. Distinct from 'siru' (marker), 'masue' (measure), 'phamori' (symptom), 'somaki' (test), and 'kithero' (diagnose).

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A finding word that retains source and method prevents an observation from silently becoming a diagnosis or overriding the person's own account.

tags.cognition: a method-bounded observation available for interpretation
tags.physical: an observation concerning bodily or health condition
